item.title: Orsted and Shell defeat lawsuit from Musk’s X
item.description: World’s richest man and Trump ally sued renewables developers and multiple other companies for allegedly conspiring to boycott advertising on social media platform X

item.title: Watershed moment beckons for Asian renewables amid Persian Gulf turmoil
item.description: Asian nations are rethinking their reliance on seaborne liquefied natural gas, and offshore wind could be the winner

item.title: Vestas stocks surge after UK sends 'important signal' with Mingyang factory snub
item.description: UK government rejects Mingyang plan for a Scottish offshore wind turbine factory and bars turbines from British waters, leaving Vestas and Siemens Gamesa to split market between them

item.title: Scottish leaders say Mingyang factory snub is 'sabotaging industrial future'
Octopus blasts UK government over Chinese wind turbine shutout
item.description: Country's largest power retailer says 'it shouldn’t be beyond British ingenuity to secure wind turbines' after decision to bar Mingyang factory

item.title: Crown Estate unveils plans for new UK offshore wind leasing round
item.description: Seabed landlord for England & Wales unveils plans for new leasing round focused predominantly on waters in the northeast of England

item.title: Vestas gives UK what Mingyang cannot as Chinese wind giant snubbed
item.description: ‘No coincidence’ that Vestas announced new Scottish offshore wind turbine factory plan on same day news leaked that Mingyang proposal was rejected

item.title: UK government rejects Mingyang plan for Scottish factory
item.description: Hotly anticipated decision comes amid fierce debate over whether Chinese wind turbines pose a security threat to Europe

item.title: Brookfield and La Caisse in massive deal to buy Canada's Boralex
item.description: All-Canadian transaction aims at helping utility to grow faster in North American and European core markets

item.title: TotalEnergies boss delivers stinging verdict on US offshore wind after exit
item.description: Boss of French supermajor calls US offshore wind overly expensive and a 'marginal' technology and says other developers could look to strike Trump exit deal

item.title: Germany to hold huge 'booster' wind tenders in response to Iran crisis
item.description: Energy minister Reiche insists that new wind farms on land will only be added where there is sufficient transmission capacity to avoid rising energy system costs

item.title: Vestas plans UK nacelle factory for offshore wind turbines
item.description: Plant would handle V236 15MW components subject to success in UK auctions

item.title: Vestas and Orsted shares rise despite 'very muddy election outcome' in Denmark
item.description: Moderate Party of foreign minister could become kingmaker in either a centre-left of centre-right government, and reject more radical proposals

item.title: Nordex debuts turbine with very high tower to tap into very low winds
item.description: Exceptional hub height to make sure that 7MW machines will generate electricity even at very lower wind speeds, German manufacturer says

item.title: Cadeler boss lands record bonus on soaring profits for vessel operator
item.description: Leading supplier of offshore wind turbine installation vessels enjoyed bumper 2025 with soaring revenues and doubling of fleet

item.title: US energy storage breaks installation record
item.description: Despite record installations and a positive trajectory, 52GW of energy storage hangs on regulatory uncertainty and variable demand growth

item.title: Spain's blackout blame game just won't stop – but at least wind and solar are off the hook
item.description: A long-awaited report hasn't stopped Spain's politicians and power executives slinging mud over the causes of the massive 2025 outage

item.title: Cadeler boss hails defining year for vessel operator as earnings surge
item.description: Top supplier of next-generation offshore wind installation vessels sees O&M accounting for half of its turnover one day

item.title: Why Trump's Total buyout sets 'dangerous precedent' for offshore wind
item.description: Future where renewable energy projects can be halted through political buyouts could stunt development and decarbonisation, it is warned

item.title: As Trump's offshore wind war rages, third stopped project powers up
item.description: Dominion Energy announced that its 2.6GW Virginia project has begun feeding the grid “right on schedule” despite stop-work order

item.title: Trump’s 'political theatre' won’t derail offshore wind's trajectory: industry group
item.description: At-sea renewables advocate Oceantic Network lambasts administration for giving away a billion in taxpayer dollars to feed President’s vendetta

item.title: TotalEnergies takes Trump offer to put offshore wind funds into gas
item.description: Nearly $1bn refund marks latest effort by administration to destroy once burgeoning industry and redirects French oil major's investments into natural gas

item.title: TotalEnergies quits US offshore wind after accepting nearly $1bn Trump gas buyout offer
item.description: Nearly $1bn refund marks latest effort by administration to destroy once burgeoning industry and redirects French oil major's investments into natural gas

item.title: EU OKs novel Danish offshore wind support scheme – but legal threat looms
item.description: European Commission approves $5.8bn novel aid scheme for two offshore wind sites that developers of onshore wind and solar claim is illegal under EU law and have pledged to fight

item.title: Three dead after wind turbine blaze
item.description: Wind farm in South Korea had reportedly only recently suffered a turbine collapse

item.title: Slump in German onshore tender price pressures developers and manufacturers
item.description: Winning bids reach levels that threatens developer profits and could re-ignite price war among wind turbine manufacturers

item.title: Denmark election: What's on the line for Orsted, Vestas and wind industry
item.description: Right-wing parties are pushing for government to sell stake in Orsted and are sceptical of proposed energy island linking to offshore wind farms

item.title: Offshore wind could drive billions in US steelmaking – if it ever gets built
item.description: Industry group highlights $42bn steelmaking upside but comes as administration is reportedly willing to refund developers to walk away from offshore wind projects

item.title: Deals of the Week: Nordex double in core market; UK onshore acquisition
UK military orders new radars that can live with offshore wind
item.description: Industry group says order could clear path for 10GW of projects
